channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli doctors say five released Thai hostages in 'fair' health after 15 months of captivity
item.description: BEER YAAKOV, Israel (AP) — When the mother of one of the Thai hostages held in the Gaza Strip for over a year caught sight of her son on a Facebook livestream after his release Thursday, he had changed so much that she didn’t recognize him at first.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama's president says there will be no negotiation about ownership of canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama President José Raúl Mulino said Thursday there will be no negotiation with the United States over ownership of the Panama Canal, and he hopes U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Study finds India doubled its tiger population in a decade and credits conservation efforts
item.description: India doubled its tiger population in a little over a decade by protecting the big cats from poaching and habitat loss, ensuring they have enough prey, reducing human-wildlife conflict, and increasing communities’ living standards near tiger areas.
item.dc:creator: Sibi Arasu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband, imprisoned for raping and drugging her, now caught up in other cases
item.description: NANTERRE, France (AP) — Dominique Pélicot, the convicted rapist who horrified France by drugging his then wife so other men could rape her, was questioned Thursday about other cases of rape and murder that he's suspected in.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS GARRIGA and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Moment IDF soldier is reunited with family seen on video
item.description: Agam Berger, an IDF soldier who was taken hostage during Hamas' attacks on Oct. 7, 2023, is reunited with her family after spending 482 days in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European Central bank cuts benchmark rate by a quarter percentage point to boost stagnant economy
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — The European Central Bank cut its key interest rate on Thursday to boost an economy that’s struggling to grow as consumers burned by inflation warily eye price tags and businesses try to navigate political turmoil in leading economies France and Germany.
item.dc:creator: DAVID McHUGH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Albanians pay last respects to Orthodox Christian Archbishop Anastasios
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Thousands of Albanians gathered in the capital on Thursday to pay their last respects to Archbishop Anastasios, who revived the country’s Orthodox Christian Church after the fall of the Communist party in 1990.
item.dc:creator: BY LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Merkel criticizes German front-runner Merz over migration vote with far-right support
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel on Thursday issued rare public criticism of her successor as the country's center-right leader, the front-runner in Germany's election next month, for putting to parliament proposals for tough new migration rules that only passed with the help of
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo say they plan to take their fight to the capital
item.description: Rwanda-backed rebels who have captured eastern Congo’s largest city say they plan to take their rebellion to the capital and seek to gain political power.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Justin Kabumba, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A New Zealand mountain is granted personhood, recognizing it as sacred for Māori
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A mountain in New Zealand considered an ancestor by Indigenous people was recognized as a legal person on Thursday after a new law granted it all the rights and responsibilities of a human being.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: Hamas-led militants freed eight more hostages from the Gaza Strip and another 110 Palestinian prisoners were released by Israel as part of a ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Joseph Krauss, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels take more towns as they expand control after seizing east Congo's largest city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rebels backed by Rwanda captured more towns in eastern Congo on Wednesday as they moved beyond the key city of Goma in an apparent attempt to expand their control in the conflict-battered region.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leader of rebels who toppled Syrian President Bashar Assad is named country's interim president
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The Syrian factions that toppled President Bashar Assad last month named an Islamist former rebel leader as the country‘s interim president on Wednesday in a push to project a united front as they face the monumental task of rebuilding Syria after nearly 14 years of civil war.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans urge Trump to sanction ICC after failed legislation push
item.description: Senate Republicans are recommending President Trump issue an executive order to sanction the International Criminal Court (ICC) for its pursuit of war crimes cases against Israel, after Senate Democrats blocked sanctions legislation absent a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Rubio exempting 'life-saving humanitarian assistance’ from foreign aid restrictions
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio issued a waiver on Tuesday exempting “life-saving humanitarian assistance” from President Trump’s executive order pausing nearly all foreign aid during a period of review. Trump’s executive order pausing funding, issued on his first day in office, suspended any new U.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Backlash may shatter President Macron's plans to leave mark on Notre Dame windows
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron’s plans for installing contemporary windows inside the restored Notre Dame Cathedral is facing furious opposition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu accepts Trump’s invite, will meet at White House next week
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and President Trump are expected to meet next week in Washington, making him the first foreign leader to visit to the White House since Trump returned to office. The White House confirmed Netanyahu had accepted the invite for the Feb. 4 meeting.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Year of the Snake is underway with festivities around the world
item.description: The holiday — known as the Spring Festival in China, Tet in Vietnam and Seollal in Korea — is a major festival celebrated around the world.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli President Herzog highlights antisemitism in UN speech as new report shows shocking trend
item.description: Israel's President Isaac Herzog addressed the U.N. on Monday at its annual Holocaust Remembrance Day event. His speech comes as a new report shows a worrying rise in antisemitic attitudes globally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage Naama Levy breaks silence in first message since her release
item.description: Naama Levy, one of the four IDF soldiers released from Hamas captivity on Saturday, writes a message of gratitude in her first statement since arriving home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US consulate warns of gun battles, IEDs, kidnappings in Mexican border towns near Texas
item.description: U.S. officials have put several Mexican border towns near Texas under the highest-level travel advisory amid gun battles, kidnappings and IEDs in the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A weakened Iran has Iraq looking to curb Tehran-backed extremists in country
item.description: The Iraqi government is looking to gain control of Iranian-aligned armed groups that operate inside of Iraq but outside state control, in yet another sign of Iran's weakened position since October 7.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In ‘2000 Meters to Andriivka,’ Oscar winner takes viewers back to Ukraine’s front lines
item.description: Two years after “20 Days in Mariupol” debuted in Park City, Utah, Oscar and Pulitzer Prize winner Mstyslav Chernov is back at the Sundance Film Festival with a new dispatch from Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Airliner's final 4 minutes of recordings are missing after crash that killed 179: investigators
item.description: The black boxes of a plane that crashed in South Korea last month failed to record the final four minutes of the flight before the crash that killed 179 people on board, officials said in a preliminary report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Romanian prehistoric gold treasures stolen from Dutch museum
item.description: The Geto-Dacian artifacts belonged to the National History Museum in the capital Bucharest and were on loan to the Drents Museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
